• Main Page
  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples
  • File List
  • Globals

File List

Here is a list of all documented files with brief descriptions:
mde-version.hVersion information for the Multicore Development Environment as a whole
arch/abi.hABI-related register definitions
arch/atomic.hProvides macros for common atomic functionality
arch/cycle.hInterface to the "cycle count", which is a 64-bit number, initialized to zero at boot, and incremented at the end of each machine cycle
arch/icache.hSupport for invalidating bytes in the instruction cache
arch/idn.hLow level support for the IDN
arch/sim.hProvides an API for controlling the simulator at runtime
arch/sim_def.hSome low-level simulator definitions
arch/udn.hProvides low-level support for the UDN (user dynamic network)
gxcr/aead.hAn API for hardware-accelerated AEAD packet processing
gxcr/gxcr.hAn API for using hardware-accelerated cryptographic functions
gxcr/ipsec.hAn API for hardware-accelerated IPsec packet processing
gxcr/pka.hAn API for interfacing to the Tilera MiCA Public Key Hardware
gxio/common.hRoutines shared between the various GXIO device components
gxio/gpio.hAn API for manipulating general-purpose I/O pins
gxio/mica.hAn API for allocating and manipulating MiCA hardware resources
gxio/mica_queue.hAn API for allocating and manipulating MiCA Queues
gxio/mpipe.hAn API for allocating, configuring, and manipulating mPIPE hardware resources
gxio/pka.hPublic Key Authentication Acceleration and True Random Number Generator MiCA driver support functions
gxio/trio.hAn API for allocating, configuring, and manipulating TRIO hardware resources
gxio/usb_dev.hAn API for manipulating the USB device shim
hv/drv_mica_intf.hInterface definitions for the general mica driver
hv/drv_mpipe_intf.hInterface definitions for the mpipe driver
tmc/alloc.hRoutines for allocating pages with specified home cache, memory controller, and length
tmc/cmem.hAdditional cmem ("common memory") APIs for tmc_shmem
tmc/cpus.hCPU management and task affinitization
tmc/interrupt.hRoutines for installing a user-space interrupt with a specific interrupt number and its user-space C callback handler
tmc/ipi.hSupport for user-level IPI event handling
tmc/malloc.hThe malloc replacement library using tmc_mspace
tmc/mem.hSupport for moving blocks of memory in and out of a Tile's cache
tmc/mspace.hThe mspace parameterized heap API
tmc/perf.hSupport accessing perf counters from user space
tmc/queue.hMacro-based support for shared memory FIFOs
tmc/queue_internal.hShared definitions for the various TMC queue implementations
tmc/shmem.hInter-process dynamic shared memory support
tmc/spin.hSpin-based memory synchronization primitives for use with one-task-per-core applications
tmc/sync.hRoutines for performing cross-process shared memory synchronization without requiring pthreads
tmc/task.hTask management and cleanup
tmc/udn.hSupport for the UDN (user dynamic network)

The following are trademarks of Tilera Corporation: Embedding Multicore, The Multicore Company, Tile Processor, TILE Architecture, TILE64, TILEPro, TILEPro36, TILEPro64, TILExpress, TILExpress-64, TILExpressPro-64, TILExpress-20G, TILExpressPro-20G, TILExpressPro-22G, iMesh, TileDirect, TILEmpower, TILEmpower-Gx, TILEncore, TILEncorePro, TILEncore-Gx, TILE-Gx, TILE-Gx16, TILE-Gx36, TILE-Gx64, TILE-Gx100, TILE-Gx3000, TILE-Gx5000, TILE-Gx8000, DDC (Dynamic Distributed Cache), Multicore Development Environment, Gentle Slope Programming, iLib, TMC (Tilera Multicore Components), hardwall, Zero Overhead Linux (ZOL), MiCA (Multicore iMesh Coprocessing Accelerator), and mPIPE (multicore Programmable Intelligent Packet Engine). All other trademarks and/or registered trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

Third-party software: The Tilera IDE makes use of the BeanShell scripting library. Source code for the BeanShell library can be found at the BeanShell website (http://www.beanshell.org/developer.html).

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).
